meta-platforms.summary: €2.52B cumulative GDPR fines: €1.2B (May 2023, EU–US transfers), €390M (Jan 2023, ads consent), €405M (Sep 2022, Instagram children), €265M (Nov 2022, data scraping), €251M (Dec 2024, breach notification).

amazon.summary: €746M Luxembourg CNPD fine (Jul 2021) for processing personal data for advertising without valid consent — largest GDPR fine at the time.

bytedance.summary: €875M GDPR fines on TikTok: €530M Irish DPC (May 2025) for unlawful EU user data transfers to China, plus €345M (Sep 2023) over children's privacy defaults.

google-alphabet.summary: €140M+ in GDPR/ePrivacy fines including €90M CNIL (Jan 2022) and €50M CNIL (2019) for cookie consent and transparency violations.

uber.summary: €290M Dutch DPA fine (Aug 2024) for transferring EU driver data to US servers without adequate safeguards.

marriott-international.summary: £18.4M (€22M) UK ICO fine (Oct 2020) for the Starwood breach exposing 339M guest records.

spotify.summary: SEK 58M (€5M) Swedish IMY fine (Jun 2023) for failing to properly inform users about how their data is processed when they exercise GDPR access rights.
